# @codluv/versionguard
> Strict versioning enforcement for SemVer and CalVer — language-agnostic manifest support, git hooks, changelog validation, and file sync
> Version: 0.9.0

## Install

```bash
npm install -D @codluv/versionguard
```

## Documentation

- [API Reference](./api-reference.mdx): Full API documentation (MDX)
- [Full Context](./llms-full.txt): Complete symbol documentation for deep LLM consumption

## Packages

### main
95 functions, 60 types

- getSemVerConfig() — Resolves the SemVer config from a VersionGuard config.
- getCalVerConfig() — Extracts the CalVer config from a VersionGuard config, throwing if missing.
- validateModifier() — Validates a pre-release / modifier tag against the allowed modifiers list.
- isValidCalVerFormat() — Validates that a CalVer format string is composed of valid tokens and follows structural rules.
- parseFormat() — Breaks a CalVer format string into its component tokens.
- getRegexForFormat() — Builds a regular expression that matches a supported CalVer format.
- parse() — Parses a CalVer string using the supplied format.
- validate() — Validates a CalVer string against formatting and date rules.
- format() — Formats a parsed CalVer object back into a version string.
- getCurrentVersion() — Creates the current CalVer string for a format.
- compare() — Compares two CalVer strings using a shared format.
- increment() — Increments a CalVer string.
- getNextVersions() — Returns the most likely next CalVer candidates.
- validateChangelog() — Validates a changelog file for release readiness.
- getLatestVersion() — Gets the most recent released version from a changelog.
- addVersionEntry() — Inserts a new version entry beneath the `[Unreleased]` section.
- isChangesetMangled() — Detects whether a changelog has been mangled by Changesets.
- fixChangesetMangling() — Fixes a Changesets-mangled changelog into proper Keep a Changelog format.
- createCkmEngine() — Creates a CKM engine from a parsed manifest.
- parse() — Parses a semantic version string.
- validate() — Validates that a string is a supported semantic version.
- compare() — Compares two semantic version strings.
- gt() — Checks whether one semantic version is greater than another.
- lt() — Checks whether one semantic version is less than another.
- eq() — Checks whether two semantic versions are equal in precedence.
- increment() — Increments a semantic version string by release type.
- format() — Formats a parsed semantic version object.
- getVersionFeedback() — Generates actionable feedback for a version string.
- getSyncFeedback() — Generates suggestions for version sync mismatches in a file.
- getChangelogFeedback() — Generates suggestions for changelog-related validation issues.
- getTagFeedback() — Generates suggestions for git tag mismatches.
- getNestedValue() — Traverses a nested object using a dotted key path.
- setNestedValue() — Sets a value at a dotted key path, throwing if intermediate segments are missing.
- escapeRegExp() — Escapes special regex characters in a string for safe use in `new RegExp()`.
- resolveVersionSource() — Resolves the version source provider for a project.
- detectManifests() — Detects all manifest files present in a project directory.
- getPackageJsonPath() — Gets the `package.json` path for a project directory.
- readPackageJson() — Reads and parses a project's `package.json` file.
- writePackageJson() — Writes a `package.json` document back to disk.
- getPackageVersion() — Gets the version string from the project manifest.  When a `manifest` config is provided, uses the configured version source provider (auto-detection or explicit). Falls back to `package.json` for backwards compatibility when no config is provided.
- setPackageVersion() — Sets the version field in the project manifest.  When a `manifest` config is provided, uses the configured version source provider. Falls back to `package.json` for backwards compatibility when no config is provided.
- getVersionSource() — Resolves the version source provider for a project.
- syncVersion() — Synchronizes configured files to a single version string.
- syncFile() — Synchronizes a single file to a target version.
- checkHardcodedVersions() — Checks configured files for hardcoded version mismatches.
- scanRepoForVersions() — Scans the entire repository for hardcoded version literals.
- fixPackageVersion() — Updates the `package.json` version field when needed.
- fixSyncIssues() — Synchronizes configured files to the package version.
- fixChangelog() — Ensures the changelog contains an entry for a version.
- fixAll() — Runs all configured auto-fix operations.
- suggestNextVersion() — Suggests candidate next versions for a release.
- generateDependabotConfig() — Generates Dependabot YAML configuration from detected manifests.
- writeDependabotConfig() — Writes a Dependabot configuration file to `.github/dependabot.yml`.
- dependabotConfigExists() — Checks whether `.github/dependabot.yml` exists in the project.
- installHooks() — Installs VersionGuard-managed Git hooks in a repository.
- uninstallHooks() — Removes VersionGuard-managed Git hooks from a repository.
- findGitDir() — Finds the nearest `.git` directory by walking up from a starting directory.
- areHooksInstalled() — Checks whether all VersionGuard-managed hooks are installed.
- generateHookScript() — Generates the shell script content for a Git hook.
- checkHooksPathOverride() — Checks whether git hooks have been redirected away from the repository.
- checkHuskyBypass() — Checks whether the HUSKY environment variable is disabling hooks.
- checkHookIntegrity() — Verifies that installed hook scripts match the expected content.
- checkEnforceHooksPolicy() — Checks whether hooks are configured as required but not enforced.
- runGuardChecks() — Runs all guard checks and returns a consolidated report.
- checkPublishStatus() — Checks whether a package version has been published to its ecosystem registry.
- readPackageName() — Reads the package name from a manifest file for registry lookups.
- getDefaultConfig() — Returns a deep-cloned copy of the built-in VersionGuard configuration.
- findConfig() — Finds the first supported VersionGuard config file in a directory.
- loadConfig() — Loads a VersionGuard config file from disk.
- getConfig() — Resolves the active VersionGuard configuration for a project.
- initConfig() — Initializes a new VersionGuard config file in a project.
- findProjectRoot() — Walks up from `startDir` to find the nearest project root.
- formatNotProjectError() — Formats a helpful error message when a command can't find a project.
- getLatestTag() — Returns the most recent reachable git tag for a repository.
- getAllTags() — Lists all tags in a repository.
- createTag() — Creates a release tag and optionally fixes version state first.
- handlePostTag() — Runs post-tag validation and sync checks.
- validateTagForPush() — Validates that a local tag is safe to push to the default remote.
- suggestTagMessage() — Suggests an annotated tag message from changelog content.
- validateVersion() — Validates a version string against the active versioning strategy.
- validate() — Validates the current project state against the supplied configuration.
- doctor() — Runs an extended readiness check for a project.
- sync() — Synchronizes configured files to the current package version.
- canBump() — Determines whether a project can move from one version to another.
- runWizard() — Runs the interactive setup wizard.
- runHeadless() — Initializes VersionGuard non-interactively using CLI flags.
- createProgram() — Creates the VersionGuard CLI program definition.
- runCli() — Parses CLI arguments and executes the matching command.
- shouldRunCli() — Determines whether the current module is the invoked CLI entry point.
- createTempProject() — Creates a temporary project directory with a minimal `package.json` fixture.
- initGitRepo() — Initializes a git repository in a fixture directory with a first commit.
- writeTextFile() — Writes a text fixture file relative to a temporary project directory.
- createBareRemote() — Creates a bare git repository to use as a remote in integration tests.
- addGitRemote() — Adds a local bare repository as the `origin` remote for a fixture repo.
- commitAll() — Stages all changes and creates a commit in a fixture repository.

- VersioningType — Supported versioning strategies.
- ManifestSourceType — Supported manifest source types for version extraction.
- ManifestConfig — Configures the version source manifest.
- CalVerToken — Valid CalVer token names for building format strings.
- CalVerFormat — A CalVer format string composed of dot-separated tokens.
- SchemeRules — Configures scheme-level validation rules applied regardless of versioning type.
- SemVerConfig — Configures SemVer validation rules.
- CalVerConfig — Configures CalVer validation rules.
- SyncPattern — Describes a search-and-replace pattern used during version synchronization.
- SyncConfig — Configures files and patterns that should stay in sync with the canonical version.
- ChangelogConfig — Controls changelog validation behavior.
- GitHooksConfig — Toggles each supported git hook integration.
- GitConfig — Configures git-related enforcement.
- GitHubConfig — Configures GitHub-specific integration features.
- VersioningConfig — Configures the active versioning mode.
- ScanAllowlistEntry — An intentional version reference that should be excluded from scan results.
- ScanConfig — Configures repo-wide scanning for hardcoded version literals.
- GuardConfig — Configures guard check behavior (hook bypass detection).
- PublishConfig — Configures registry publish status verification.
- PublishCheckResult — Result of a registry publish status check.
- GuardWarning — Describes a single guard finding.
- GuardReport — Result of a full guard check pass.
- ValidateMode — Controls whether validate runs all checks or a fast subset.
- VersionGuardConfig — Top-level configuration consumed by versionguard.
- SemVer — Parsed semantic version components.
- CalVer — Parsed calendar version components.
- ParsedSemVer — Parsed semantic version result wrapper.
- ParsedCalVer — Parsed calendar version result wrapper.
- ParsedVersion — Union of supported parsed version payloads.
- ValidationError — Describes a single validation problem.
- ValidationResult — Result returned by version parsing and validation helpers.
- SyncChange — Describes a single in-file version replacement.
- SyncResult — Reports the result of synchronizing a single file.
- VersionMismatch — Reports a discovered version mismatch.
- FullValidationResult — Combined result from a full project validation run.
- DoctorReport — Reports whether a project is ready to pass VersionGuard checks.
- ParsedCalVerFormat — Parsed token layout for a supported CalVer format string.
- ChangelogValidationResult — Describes the outcome of validating a changelog file.
- ChangelogStructureOptions — Options for changelog structure enforcement.
- CkmConcept — A domain concept extracted from an exported interface or type.
- CkmProperty — A property within a concept.
- CkmOperation — A user-facing operation extracted from an exported function.
- CkmInput — A function parameter within an operation.
- CkmConstraint — A constraint enforced by the tool.
- CkmWorkflow — A multi-step workflow for a common goal.
- CkmConfigEntry — A config schema entry with type, description, and default.
- CkmManifest — The complete Codebase Knowledge Manifest.
- CkmTopic — An auto-generated topic derived from CKM data.
- CkmEngine — The CKM engine — provides topic derivation, filtering, and output formatting.
- Suggestion — Feedback entry point exports for suggestion and guidance helpers.
- FeedbackResult — Aggregates validation errors with suggested next steps.
- VersionSourceProvider — Abstraction for reading and writing a version string from any manifest format.
- PackageJsonValue — JSON-compatible scalar, array, or object value used by package metadata.
- PackageJsonArray — Recursive array type used for arbitrary JSON-compatible package values.
- PackageJsonObject — Recursive object type used for arbitrary JSON-compatible package values.
- PackageJson — Minimal shape of a `package.json` document used by VersionGuard.
- FixResult — Fix entry point exports for auto-remediation helpers.
- ProjectRootResult — Result of project root detection.
- TagInfo — Tag entry point exports for release-tag management helpers.
- InitOptions — Options for headless (non-interactive) initialization.
